title: Bunchosia media
taxonomy: Bunchosia media is classified within the family Melastomataceae. This family consists of approximately 170 genera and over 5,000 species of flowering plants. Bunchosia media is also classified as a member of the subfamily Miconieae. The subfamily Miconieae comprises several genera of flowering plants native to the Americas, primarily in Central and South America. As a member of this subfamily, Bunchosia media exhibits characteristics typical of its family and subfamily.

side_effects: Bunchosia media, also known as the Panama rose, is not typically associated with any severe side effects when used appropriately. intro: Bunchosia media, also known as the Amazonian tree tomato, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Bunchosia media include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
